title: Light Years Traveled
query: SELECT CAST(COALESCE(SUM(JumpDist), 0) AS INTEGER) AS total, COUNT(*) AS jumps FROM FSDJump WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {jumps} hyperspace jump(s) through folded space covering {total} ly during the report period. For context, the Milky Way is approximately 100,000 ly across and Sagittarius A* sits roughly 26,000 ly from the bubble.
details.help: JumpDist is in light years per jump. Cast to integer for display. summaryOnly: in a by-system report this would trivially return 1 jump.

tagLines: Long-range displacement metrics updated. Pilots Federation travel registry amended accordingly.
That's a lot of hyperspace jumps. You'll never get those seconds back.
The galaxy is 100,000 light years wide. You are closing the distance, one jump at a time.
